{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47353","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47353/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47353/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47353/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/47353","id":1426099911,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM5BsOGL","number":47353,"title":"Define `length(::Flatten)` for some collections of arrays","user":{"login":"mcabbott","id":32575566,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMyNTc1NTY2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/32575566?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mcabbott","html_url":"https://github.com/mcabbott","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mcabbott/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mcabbott/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mcabbott/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mcabbott/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mcabbott/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mcabbott/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mcabbott/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mcabbott/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mcabbott/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":815495023,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw4MTU0OTUwMjM=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/iteration","name":"iteration","color":"a098ea","default":false,"description":"Involves iteration or the iteration protocol"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2022-10-27T18:30:43Z","updated_at":"2024-11-29T03:19:36Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/47353","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/47353","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/47353.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/47353.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"
As suggested at https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/23431, this defines `length` (and `IteratorSize` trait) for a few more `Iterators.flatten` objects: those flattening an arrays of array, or a tuples of arrays.

Computing the total length involves adding the lengths of the constituent arrays. At present `length(::Flatten)` never does this. For these cases it seems to be very cheap.

Knowing the length speeds up `collect ∘ Iterators.flatten`, although there is still room for improvement:
```julia
julia> vecs = [rand(100) for _ in 1:100];

julia> @btime length(Iterators.flatten($vecs))
  min 54.133 ns, mean 54.270 ns (0 allocations)  # this PR
10000

julia> @btime collect(Iterators.flatten($vecs));
  min 68.875 μs, mean 81.634 μs (9 allocations, 326.55 KiB) # master
  min 12.167 μs, mean 17.848 μs (2 allocations, 78.17 KiB)  # this PR

julia> @btime reduce(vcat, $vecs);
  min 2.403 μs, mean 8.332 μs (3 allocations, 79.05 KiB)  # ideal?
  
julia> tvecs = Tuple(rand(1000) for _ in 1:10);

julia> @btime collect(Iterators.flatten($tvecs));
  min 69.167 μs, mean 81.648 μs (9 allocations, 326.55 KiB)  # master
  min 12.833 μs, mean 22.149 μs (2 allocations, 78.17 KiB)   # this PR

julia> @btime vcat($tvecs...);
  min 1.780 μs, mean 11.348 μs (2 allocations, 78.17 KiB)  # ideal?
```
Some other cases with already known length remain quite slow:
```julia
julia> tups = [Tuple(rand(10)) for _ in 1:1000];

julia> Base.haslength(tups)  # unchanged, uses eltype of array
true

julia> v = @btime collect(Iterators.flatten($tups));
  min 117.583 μs, mean 131.905 μs (2 allocations, 78.17 KiB)

julia> v == vec(@btime stack($tups))
  min 8.597 μs, mean 18.170 μs (2 allocations, 78.17 KiB)
true
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47353/reactions","total_count":2,"+1":2,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47353/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2507021153","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/47353#issuecomment-2507021153","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47353","id":2507021153,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6Vbhth","user":{"login":"fredcallaway","id":7883156,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc4ODMxNTY=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/7883156?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway","html_url":"https://github.com/fredcallaway","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-29T03:19:35Z","updated_at":"2024-11-29T03:19:35Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Bump on this. From a user perspective, it seems really weird that length(::Flatten) doesn't work when flattening any iterators that have known length. I think it shouldn't be limited to arrays and tuples though, e.g. UnitRange and CartesianIndices should work as well. From my perspective, it's about semantics, not just efficiency for things like map.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2507021153/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48777","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48777/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48777/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48777/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48777","id":1598520781,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5fR33N","number":48777,"title":"`unique` not working for vector of tuple types","user":{"login":"manuelbb-upb","id":58472644,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjU4NDcyNjQ0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/58472644?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/manuelbb-upb","html_url":"https://github.com/manuelbb-upb","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/manuelbb-upb/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/manuelbb-upb/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/manuelbb-upb/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/manuelbb-upb/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/manuelbb-upb/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/manuelbb-upb/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/manuelbb-upb/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/manuelbb-upb/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/manuelbb-upb/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2023-02-24T12:08:57Z","updated_at":"2024-03-14T16:14:12Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"According to the [docs](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1/base/collections/#Base.unique), `unique` should filter based on `isequal`.
This does not work for iterable objects of tuple types:
```julia
T1 = Tuple{T} where T<:Real
T2 = Tuple{Real}

isequal(T1, T2) == true
allequal([T1, T2]) == true
unique([T1,T2]) == [T1, T2] # would expect singleton [T1] or [T2] here
allunique([T1, T2]) == true # also seems contradictory
```

**versioninfo**
```
Julia Version 1.8.5
Commit 17cfb8e65ea (2023-01-08 06:45 UTC)
Platform Info:
  OS: Linux (x86_64-linux-gnu)
  CPU: 8 × 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-1165G7 @ 2.80GHz
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LIBM: libopenlibm
  LLVM: libLLVM-13.0.1 (ORCJIT, tigerlake)
  Threads: 4 on 8 virtual cores
Environment:
  JULIA_NUM_THREADS = 4
  LD_PRELOAD = /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libstdc++.so.6
```
Downloaded the latest binary for testing.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48777/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48777/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1997824539","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48777#issuecomment-1997824539","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/48777","id":1997824539,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM53FGIb","user":{"login":"fredcallaway","id":7883156,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc4ODMxNTY=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/7883156?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway","html_url":"https://github.com/fredcallaway","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/fredcallaway/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-14T16:14:11Z","updated_at":"2024-03-14T16:14:11Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I just ran into a similar issue for a custom struct. I think it's a documentation bug. The docs of `unique` should clarify that equality is determined by hash so that end users know they need to override both `hash` and `isequal`. I realize that the docs for `isequal` state that `isequal` should imply `hash` equality but should we expect the user to know/remember this?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1997824539/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
